sourceMovie.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Resident Evil: Degeneration for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Makoto Kamiya keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Degeneration clicked for you, Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Cowboy Bebop: The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Degeneration clicked for you, Cowboy Bebop: The Movie (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Resident Evil: Degeneration?
faq.answer: Try Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust, Cowboy Bebop: The Movie, Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon a Time—each shares DNA with Resident Evil: Degeneration while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Resident Evil: Degeneration?
faq.answer: Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is best described as Animation, Horror, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Resident Evil: Degeneration part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Resident Evil (Animated) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
